mnemonics: DDL DML DCL DQL - Dad Makes Delicious Chocolate Cake (Data Definition, Manipulation, Control, Query Languages)
keyPoints: DDL (Data Definition Language) creates/modifies database structure
DML (Data Manipulation Language) handles data operations like INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE
Three-schema architecture separates physical, conceptual, and external views
commonMistakes: Confusing DDL (structure changes) with DML (data changes)
Mixing up conceptual schema (logical design) with physical schema (storage details)
Forgetting that DCL (Data Control Language) handles security/permissions
